title: Multi-Actor Service Discovery and Conflicting Outcomes · Evidence Recall
summary: A delayed review reconstructs multi-actor service discovery and conflicting outcomes from stakeholder, decision, assumption, revision, artifact, observation, changed case, rejected failure, repair, owner, and residual-risk evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-actor service map and conflict register after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map direct users, indirect users, operators, approvers, maintainers, adversaries, and affected non-users with their decisions and dependencies.
Trace frontstage and backstage actions, handoffs, wait states, channels, policies, evidence, and breakdowns across an end-to-end service.
Expose conflicting speed, control, privacy, accessibility, workload, cost, and accountability outcomes instead of averaging them away.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit
steps.instruction:
review case — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.

Inspect the Research Saturation Limit trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit, keep Research Saturation Limit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Research Saturation Limit, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Research Saturation Limit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Research Saturation Limit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A fixed interview count guarantees discovery saturation.

stimulus.title: Research Saturation Limit case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The review variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A fixed interview count guarantees discovery saturation.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing. Artifact under review: multi-actor service map and conflict register.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Research Saturation Limit, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort · Review Research Saturation Limit
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Research Saturation Limit: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — End users, operators, administrators, support staff, and affected non-users disagree about speed, control, workload, privacy, and recourse. The review variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.

Evidence target: Record what required effort · Review Research Saturation Limit.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Record what required effort · Review Research Saturation Limit, keep Research Saturation Limit connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Judge when additional research is repeating known patterns, where evidence is still weak, and which groups remain missing.
